title: Support elaboration `$info(%p)` and avoid Unexpected Call
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

```
module pipe_id_match #(
  parameter int unsigned     N_STAGES    = 4,            
  parameter int unsigned     N_IDS_PER_STAGE = 2,            
  parameter type             ID_T        = logic[1:0],
  parameter ID_T             STAGE_IDS[N_STAGES-1:0][N_IDS_PER_STAGE-1:0]
) (
  input  logic                clk,
  input  logic                rst_n  
);
   generate
      $info("%m %s:%0d: N_STAGES=%0d N_IDS_PER_STAGE=%0d STAGE_IDS=%p" , "test.sv",25, N_STAGES, N_IDS_PER_STAGE, STAGE_IDS);
   endgenerate
endmodule  
module tree_connect #(
  parameter int unsigned   N_IDS           = 16,          
  parameter int unsigned   N_IDS_PER_STAGE = 2,          
  parameter type           ID_T            = logic[3:0],
  parameter ID_T           SRC_ID                        
) (
  input  logic             clk,
  input  logic             rst_n
);
   localparam int unsigned N_STAGES = (N_IDS / 2) / N_IDS_PER_STAGE;
   function automatic ID_T[N_STAGES-1:0][N_IDS_PER_STAGE-1:0] gen_stage_ids ();
      for (int unsigned st=0; st < N_STAGES; st++) begin
          for (int unsigned id=0; id < N_IDS_PER_STAGE; id++) begin
             gen_stage_ids[st][id] = SRC_ID + ((st+1)*N_IDS_PER_STAGE) + id -
                                    (SRC_ID + ((st+1)*N_IDS_PER_STAGE) + id <  N_IDS ? 0 :
                                                                       N_IDS);
          end
      end
   endfunction
   localparam ID_T      STAGE_IDS[N_STAGES-1:0][N_IDS_PER_STAGE-1:0] = gen_stage_ids();
   pipe_id_match #(
      .N_STAGES       ( N_STAGES ),
      .N_IDS_PER_STAGE( N_IDS_PER_STAGE ),
      .ID_T       ( ID_T ),
      .STAGE_IDS  ( STAGE_IDS )
   ) pipe (  
   .* );
endmodule
module tb_tree_connect;
   localparam int N_IDS           = 18;
   localparam int N_IDS_PER_STAGE = 2;
   localparam int ID_W            = 5;
   typedef logic [ID_W-1:0] id_t;
   localparam id_t SRC_ID = id_t'(3);
   logic    clk, rst_n;
   tree_connect #(
      .N_IDS          ( N_IDS ),
      .N_IDS_PER_STAGE( N_IDS_PER_STAGE ),
      .ID_T           ( id_t ),
      .SRC_ID      ( SRC_ID )
   ) dut ( .* );
endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Error: test.sv:11:7: Internal: Unexpected Call
                    : ... note: In instance 'tb_tree_connect.dut.pipe'
   11 |       $info("%m %s:%0d: N_STAGES=%0d N_IDS_PER_STAGE=%0d STAGE_IDS=%p" , "[test.sv](http://test.sv/)",25, N_STAGES, N_IDS_PER_STAGE, STAGE_IDS);
      |       ^~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.041 for more assistance.
%Error: Internal Error: test.sv:11:7: ../V3AstNodeExpr.h:5620: Unexpected Call
                                    : ... note: In instance 'tb_tree_connect.dut.pipe'
   11 |       $info("%m %s:%0d: N_STAGES=%0d N_IDS_PER_STAGE=%0d STAGE_IDS=%p" , "[test.sv](http://test.sv/)",25, N_STAGES, N_IDS_PER_STAGE, STAGE_IDS);
      |       ^~~~~
                        ... This fatal error may be caused by the earlier error(s); resolve those first.
```

VCS:

```
vcs -q -full64 +vcs+lic+wait -sverilog test.sv

Note-[IEST] $info elaboration system task
msg: tb_tree_connect.dut.pipe test.sv:25: N_STAGES=4 N_IDS_PER_STAGE=2 STAGE_IDS='{'{'hc, 'hb}, '{'ha, 'h9}, '{'h8, 'h7}, '{'h6, 'h5}}
location: file test.sv line 11
path: tb_tree_connect.dut.pipe                                      


1 module and 0 UDP read.
../simv up to date
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
verilator --binary --sv -Wno-fatal test.sv
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

```
Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-140-g1432bb888
```

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
Linux 4.18.0-553.22.1.el8_10.x86_64
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
